title: The cause of the error has been fixed: "NameError: name 'help' is not…
body:
… defined"

# Before submitting

- [-] Was this discussed/approved via a Github issue? (no need for typos, doc improvements)
- [+] Did you read the [contributor guideline](https://github.com/pytorch/fairseq/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)?
- [+] Did you make sure to update the docs?
- [+] Did you write any new necessary tests?

## What does this PR do?
A typo in the code causing an error in the operation of File "fairseq\dataclass\configs.py", line 1080, in EMAConfig has been fixed
NameError: name 'help' is not defined.

## PR review
Anyone in the community is free to review the PR once the tests have passed.
If we didn't discuss your PR in Github issues there's a high chance it will not be merged.

## Did you have fun?
Make sure you had fun coding 🙃

step_plan.summary: Fix a NameError at import caused by an unquoted dict key in EMAConfig.store_ema metadata by changing metadata={help: ...} to metadata={"help": ...} in fairseq/dataclass/configs.py.

steps.operation: Quote the metadata key for store_ema in EMAConfig
steps.reason: The store_ema field currently uses an unquoted bare name help in its metadata dict (metadata={help: ...}), which triggers a NameError at module import time because Python attempts to resolve help as a variable. Quoting the key (metadata={"help": ...}) makes it a valid string key and preserves the intended metadata used for help/CLI generation.
steps.side_effects: Low risk. Fixes import-time NameError so modules depending on fairseq.dataclass.configs can be imported. No behavioral/API changes beyond correcting metadata syntax. Run import/smoke tests and existing unit tests to ensure no regressions; dependent modules that relied on failing import will now load. Lint/formatting tools (flake8/black) may flag style changes but no functional impact.
